enonce.texte: Instructions: Identify the tense used in each of the following sentences. Choose from: Present Simple, Present Continuous, Past Simple, Past Continuous, Present Perfect, Past Perfect, Future Simple (will), or Going to.

questions.texte: She usually walks to school.
They are playing football in the yard.
He visited his grandmother last weekend.
I was watching TV when the phone rang.
She has already eaten lunch.
He had left before I arrived.
I think it will rain tomorrow.
We are going to visit the museum next week.

intitule: Exercise 2: Complete with the Correct Tense

enonce.texte: Instructions: Complete the sentences with the correct tense of the verbs in brackets.

questions.texte: She (to study) ________ English every day.
Listen! The baby (to cry) ________.
I (to visit) ________ my uncle in Douala last year.
They (to play) ________ chess when I entered the room.
She (to live) ________ in Yaoundé since 2010.
When I arrived, the train (to leave) ________ already.
Don't worry! I (to help) ________ you.
Look at those clouds! It (to rain) ________.

enonce.texte: Instructions: Choose the correct tense to complete each sentence.

questions.texte: She ________ to school every morning.

sousQuestions.texte: A. is walking  B. walks  C. walked  D. has walked

questions.texte: They ________ a new house at the moment.

sousQuestions.texte: A. build  B. built  C. are building  D. have built
sousQuestions.reponse: C. are building (Present Continuous for actions in progress)

questions.texte: I ________ my grandmother yesterday.

sousQuestions.texte: A. visit  B. visited  C. have visited  D. will visit
sousQuestions.reponse: B. visited (Past Simple for completed past actions)

questions.texte: She ________ in Paris since 2015.

sousQuestions.texte: A. lived  B. lives  C. has lived  D. had lived
sousQuestions.reponse: C. has lived (Present Perfect with 'since' for duration)

questions.texte: The movie ________ already ________ when we arrived.

sousQuestions.texte: A. has / started  B. had / started  C. was / starting  D. has / been starting
sousQuestions.reponse: B. had / started (Past Perfect for an action before another past action)

questions.texte: I think it ________ tomorrow.

sousQuestions.texte: A. will rain  B. rains  C. is raining  D. is going to rain
sousQuestions.reponse: A. will rain (Future Simple for prediction)

questions.texte: She ________ a lawyer when she finishes university.

sousQuestions.texte: A. will become  B. becomes  C. is going to become  D. has become
sousQuestions.reponse: C. is going to become ('Going to' for intention/plan)

enonce.texte: Instructions: Each sentence contains a tense error. Rewrite the sentence with the correct tense.

questions.texte: He go to school every day.
questions.reponse: He goes to school every day.

sousQuestions.reponse: The subject 'He' requires the third person singular form 'goes' in the Present Simple.

questions.texte: She is playing football every Sunday.

sousQuestions.reponse: We use the Present Simple for habits and routines (every Sunday), not the Present Continuous.

questions.texte: I have seen him yesterday.

sousQuestions.reponse: We use the Past Simple for completed actions with a specific past time (yesterday), not the Present Perfect.

questions.texte: While I read, the phone rang.
questions.reponse: While I was reading, the phone rang.

sousQuestions.reponse: We use the Past Continuous for an action in progress in the past (reading) that is interrupted by another action (the phone ringing).

questions.texte: By the time we arrived, they ate all the food.
questions.reponse: By the time we arrived, they had eaten all the food.

sousQuestions.reponse: We use the Past Perfect (had eaten) to show that the action was completed before another past action (arrived).

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Read the text below and answer the questions.

"John is a secondary school student in Bafoussam. He usually wakes up at 6:00 am. He takes a shower and eats breakfast. At 7:30 am, he leaves home and walks to school. He often arrives at school at 7:45 am. His classes start at 8:00 am. Right now, it is 10:00 am and he is taking an English test. He has been studying hard for this test since last week. Yesterday, he visited his grandparents after school. He is going to go to the market with his mother this weekend."

questions.texte: What time does John usually wake up?
questions.reponse: He usually wakes up at 6:00 am.

questions.texte: What is John doing at 10:00 am?
questions.reponse: He is taking an English test.

questions.texte: Why has John been studying hard?
questions.reponse: Because he has been studying hard for this test since last week.

questions.texte: What did John do yesterday?
questions.reponse: He visited his grandparents after school.

questions.texte: What is John's plan for this weekend?
questions.reponse: He is going to go to the market with his mother this weekend.

enonce.texte: Instructions: Write a short paragraph (6-8 sentences) about your daily routine. Use at least four different tenses: Present Simple, Present Continuous, Past Simple, and Future with 'going to' or 'will'.

questions.texte: Write your paragraph here.
questions.reponse: Students' answers will vary. Example: "I usually wake up at 6:00 am. I have breakfast and go to school. Right now, I am writing my English exercise. Yesterday, I helped my mother in the kitchen. I am going to visit my friend this weekend."

sousQuestions.texte: Identify and list the tenses you used in your paragraph.
sousQuestions.reponse: Students should identify: Present Simple (wake up, have, go), Present Continuous (am writing), Past Simple (helped), Going to Future (am going to visit).

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Look at the scene described below and answer the questions using the appropriate tenses.

"It is Saturday afternoon. The family is having a picnic at the beach. Mr. Ndi is grilling fish. Mrs. Ndi is preparing a salad. The children, Sarah and James, are swimming in the water. Suddenly, it starts to rain. Everyone runs to find shelter. They will wait for the rain to stop. James has never seen such heavy rain at the beach before."

questions.texte: What is the family doing?
questions.reponse: The family is having a picnic at the beach.

questions.texte: What is Mr. Ndi doing?
What did James never see before?
questions.reponse: James has never seen such heavy rain at the beach before.

questions.texte: What will they do after the rain?
questions.reponse: They will wait for the rain to stop.

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Read the story below. Then, rewrite the story using different tenses to show the sequence of events more clearly. Pay attention to the timeline.

"Marie is a nurse at the hospital. She works very hard. At 7:00 am, she arrives at the hospital. She helps patients. She treats many sick people every day. Last week, she worked overtime. She is tired today. She is going to take a break soon."

questions.texte: Rewrite the story. Include at least one Present Perfect and one Past Perfect.
questions.reponse: Marie is a nurse at the hospital and she works very hard. She arrives at the hospital at 7:00 am where she helps patients. She has treated many sick people every day. Last week, she had already worked overtime before the weekend. She has been tired today. She is going to take a break soon.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify the tenses you used in your new version.
sousQuestions.reponse: Students should identify: Present Simple, Present Perfect (has treated), Past Perfect (had already worked), Present Perfect Continuous (has been), Going to Future, etc.
